page.content: The data presented in the table provides a comparative analysis of symptom prevalence and related metrics across different treatment groups in a clinical study. Specifically, the table highlights the incidence of nausea, with 57 participants in the duodenal balloon group reporting the symptom, representing 16.86% of that cohort, compared to 14 participants in the placebo group, or 12.61%.

page.content: The statistical significance of this difference is indicated by the P value of 0.29, suggesting no statistically significant difference in nausea incidence between the two groups. Additionally, the demographic data indicates an average age of participants, with the duodenal balloon group having a mean age of 69.0 ± 9.8 years, while the placebo group averaged 71.6 ± 12.7 years. This age distribution, ranging from 50 to 79 years for the duodenal balloon group and 40 to 85 years for the placebo group, provides essential context for interpreting the symptomatology and overall treatment outcomes as clinicians evaluate the efficacy and safety of interventions.
